items.title: Review Pipeline Orchestrator
items.why: Centralizes validation, refusal screening, collaborator injection, and stage ordering for checklist, retrieval, extraction, and final assessment across every transport adapter.
items.terminology: Application service · Dependency injection · Fail fast validation · Policy enforcement · Separation of concerns
items.interviewLine: I centralized review sequencing here so HTTP, CLI, and bridge adapters cannot drift on validation, refusal, or stage order.

items.title: Canonical Domain Schemas
items.why: Defines strict Pydantic contracts, domain enums, nested review outputs, and cross-field invariants that reject unknown fields and invalid classification or resolution combinations.
items.terminology: Data validation · Domain modeling · Design by contract · Invariant enforcement · Schema evolution
items.interviewLine: I use strict schemas and cross-field validators to make invalid domain states and silent contract drift fail immediately.

items.title: Retrieval Query Strategies
items.why: Implements interchangeable query-construction strategies, semantic-intent derivation, deterministic expansion, validated query results, and a versioned cache keyed by content and model identity.
items.terminology: Strategy pattern · Protocol oriented design · Semantic retrieval · Cache key design · Deterministic fallback
items.interviewLine: I separated query interpretation behind strategy objects so retrieval approaches can be compared without changing the retriever itself.

items.title: Grounded Summary Extraction
items.why: Converts LLM JSON into validated review summaries, normalizes payloads, applies deterministic evidence grounding, detects negation and support, and isolates the model behind a narrow protocol.
items.terminology: LLM output validation · Grounded generation · Ports and adapters · Defensive parsing · Deterministic postprocessing
items.interviewLine: I treat the model as an untrusted proposer, then validate, normalize, and ground every structured finding against the pharmacist's narrative.

items.title: Final Assessment Policy
items.why: Maps checklist evidence and reviewer-confirmed structured findings into deterministic risk lanes, classifications, scopes, escalation paths, resolution options, and rationale.
items.terminology: Decision table · Pure function · Deterministic policy · Human in the loop · Risk classification
items.interviewLine: I keep final disposition deterministic and driven by reviewer-confirmed triggers rather than letting raw model text make escalation decisions.

items.title: FastAPI Transport Boundary
items.why: Exposes validated HTTP endpoints, delegates all domain sequencing to the review pipeline, translates domain failures into status codes, reports readiness, and propagates request correlation.
items.terminology: Transport adapter · Exception translation · Request correlation · Health readiness · Thin controller
items.interviewLine: This adapter owns HTTP concerns only and delegates review behavior to the shared pipeline, preserving one domain path across transports.

items.title: Python Engine HTTP Adapter
items.why: Implements the Java-to-Python port with explicit request records, snake-case wire mapping, bounded timeouts, request-ID forwarding, health probing, response decoding, and typed exception translation.
items.terminology: Adapter pattern · Cross language contract · Serialization boundary · Timeout management · Request correlation
items.interviewLine: I isolated the cross-language HTTP contract here, including wire-name translation, timeouts, correlation headers, and typed failure handling.

items.title: RAG Wire Contract Tests
items.why: Drives the real Java adapter against an in-process capturing HTTP stub and asserts exact Python-facing JSON keys for all four cross-language RPC requests.
items.terminology: Contract testing · Cross language contract · Test double · Serialization boundary · Regression testing
items.interviewLine: These tests pin the exact JSON wire names so independent Java and Python refactors cannot silently break their shared contract.

items.title: Review Workflow State Machine
items.why: Owns typed asynchronous states and transitions for checklist, retrieval, extraction, and final assessment, including invalidation, retry, reset, demo loading, and backend-readiness guards.
items.terminology: State machine · Discriminated union · Async state management · Dependency invalidation · Separation of concerns
items.interviewLine: I modeled the UI workflow as explicit typed states and transitions so stale downstream results are invalidated when upstream inputs change.

items.title: Health-Gated Service Composition
items.why: Composes the UI, Spring API, and Python engine on one network with explicit environment configuration, service-specific health checks, and dependency startup gated on healthy upstream services.
items.terminology: Service orchestration · Health readiness · Configuration management · Dependency ordering · Container networking
items.interviewLine: I use health-gated composition so dependent services start only after their upstream boundary is actually responsive.
